place: Ramsey borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Ramsey was incorporated as a borough on March 10, 1908, carved out of Hohokus Township, now part of Mahwah.
The Old Stone House, built in the early 1700s with stone and hog’s hair, opened as a historic site in 1960.
Ramsey’s name honors Peter J. Ramsey, a 19th‑century landowner who sold the land that became a railroad station in 1848.
The Ramsey Farmers’ Market, held every Sunday from June to September, showcases local produce and crafts.
Don Bosco Preparatory High School, founded in 1915, is a private Catholic college‑prep school that has educated generations of Ramsey residents.
The Ramsey Hockey Association, a non‑profit founded to promote youth hockey, reflects the borough’s strong community sports culture.
